summary: First MVP test run of Test Automation & DevOps Practice Ground. Backend: 13/13 pytest cases pass (auth register/login/me/logout, JWT via Bearer, todos CRUD with auth, pagination shape, search/completed filters, status echo, delay ~2s, flaky 200/500, echo, sample users). Frontend: Landing → /practice with 30 cards, admin login, register+auto-login, basic-form, table-automation, modal-dialog, tabs, slider, date-picker, dynamic-list, shadow-dom, network-delay (real backend call), flaky-element, logout — all pass. ONE CRITICAL BUG: /api-playground route collides with Kubernetes ingress /api/* → backend; the page renders as FastAPI JSON 404 instead of React, breaking the entire API Playground UI feature.

minor.issue: seed_admin updates existing admin password when it changes but does NOT clear login_attempts lockout for that admin; low impact for tests.
Only sets access_token cookie, does not return refresh_token or rotate it; also does not update client-usable state on frontend since token is stored in localStorage (mismatch).

ui_bugs.issue: Route path starts with '/api' which is the same prefix that Kubernetes ingress redirects to backend port 8001. Backend returns {"detail":"Not Found"} JSON instead of serving the React SPA. Entire API Playground UI is unreachable in the preview environment. Also breaks nav-api link and hero-view-api button.

action_items: CRITICAL: Rename /api-playground route to something that does NOT start with /api (e.g. /playground, /rest-playground, /apis-lab). Update: (1) App.js Route path, (2) Layout.jsx nav-api Link, (3) Landing.jsx hero-view-api Link. Value of data-testids for run-/response- selectors can be kept as-is.
After renaming, retest the API Playground E2E: GET /playground/health returns status 200 in response block; POST /playground/todos when authenticated returns 201.
Optional: seed_admin should also delete stale login_attempts entries for admin email on startup to avoid lockout stickiness across restarts.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py hardcodes CORS allow_origins to include http://localhost:3000 and FRONTEND_URL only; if frontend URL changes, extend list. Also allow_credentials=True with a specific origin is correct — good.
server.py cookies set with secure=False; acceptable for preview but flag for production (should be True in HTTPS prod). SameSite=lax is fine.

AuthContext stores access_token in localStorage AND server sets httpOnly cookie — dual mechanism can drift. Prefer one source of truth. Current impl works because axios interceptor adds Bearer header from localStorage.
JWT_SECRET is stored in backend/.env in repo — flag for real deployment (rotate + never commit).
seed_admin uses env ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD; hash format is $2b$ (bcrypt) — verified via successful login. Good.
server.py file is 384 lines — acceptable but auth/playground/todos should ideally live in separate routers as it grows.
MongoDB _id is correctly excluded from playground endpoints (uses uuid `id` field and projects _id:0). ObjectId is stringified in public_user(). Good.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@example.com / admin123 (seeded, verified). Registration works with unique sdet+<ts>@example.com / test1234.
seed_data_creation: Admin user seeded by backend startup. Test-created todos and users are transient; cleanup handled by using unique emails / UUID todo ids.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully green via pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Only blocker is the /api-playground → backend routing collision. After main agent renames the route, re-run: (1) navigate to new route, (2) click run--playground-health, expect response--playground-health to contain 'status: 200' and 'ok'; (3) after admin login, click run--playground-todos → response--playground-todos should contain 'status: 201'. Landing page and Layout links must also point to the new path. All 30 practice section slugs load; testids follow patterns like bf-*, cb-*, dl-*, list-*, delay-*, flaky-*, tab-*, hover-*, drag-*, keyboard-*, etc.

rca_of_the_issue.bug: GET /api-playground returns {"detail":"Not Found"} (FastAPI 404) instead of the React SPA.
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Kubernetes ingress in this environment forwards ALL requests whose path begins with '/api' to the backend service on port 8001. The React route '/api-playground' matches that prefix, so the request never reaches the frontend. FastAPI has no matching endpoint and returns 404.
reproduction_steps: In browser, click 'API Playground' nav link → shows raw JSON error, not React UI
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Rename the frontend route to a path that does not start with '/api'. Suggested: '/playground', '/rest-lab', or '/api-lab' → '/apis-lab'. Update App.js Route, Layout.jsx nav Link (data-testid="nav-api" href), Landing.jsx hero-view-api href. No backend changes needed.
